verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a soluble protein lacking a transmembrane domain. It localizes to the intra-acrosomal compartment of sperm (intracellular) and is not exposed on the sperm surface even after acrosomal exocytosis, in contrast to IZUMO1 and IZUMO3. No mechanism exists for IZUMO4 to reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane from the extracellular face. While it participates in fertilization biology and shows weak functional association with the process, the protein body itself is not surface-accessible.
